explanation: This command bypasses the rule by utilizing PowerShell's syntax flexibility. By expanding aliases (-NoP to -NoProfile, -NonI to -NonInteractive, gp to Get-ItemProperty), changing the arbitrary variable name ($x to $payload), and adding quotes to the registry path, the logical execution remains identical but the strict string-matching ('contains') condition in the Sigma rule is defeated.

This bypass simply adds an extra space between parameters and includes a backslash after the HKCU drive literal (HKCU:\ instead of HKCU:). Since the Sigma rule looks for a highly specific, static string of characters with exact spacing, these trivial whitespace and syntax variations will evade detection.
